content: Just as Merriam-Webster defines an agent as any entity that exerts power to produce an effect, 2025’s episodic memory transformer agents can reframe the general definition of 'agent' to include distributed, memory-augmented AI entities where past episodic traces stored in transformer key-value weights themselves act as individual sub-agents that produce downstream effects. Each stored episodic memory in the transformer’s key-value cache functions as an independent causal agent that shapes the model’s output, rather than being a passive data store.
